description: Add round-robin proxy group data model, API endpoints, and backend resolution logic. This adds the concept of 'proxy groups' (round-robin groups) to the proxy pool system. Each group contains multiple proxy pool entries and has a mode: 'roundrobin' (rotate through proxies on each request) or 'sticky' (stick to one proxy for N requests, configurable). A 'default' group is auto-created. The resolution chain changes: when a provider/connection is assigned to a round-robin group, the system picks the next proxy from that group according to its mode. Strict proxy mode is inherited: if the selected proxy fails and strictProxy is false, fallback to direct; if strictProxy is true, fail the request.
steps: Step 1: Add ProxyGroup data model to src/lib/localDb/misc.ts. Add CRUD functions: getProxyGroups, getProxyGroupById, createProxyGroup, updateProxyGroup, deleteProxyGroup. Each ProxyGroup has: id (uuid), name (string, 'default' for the auto-created one), mode ('roundrobin' | 'sticky'), stickyLimit (number, default 1, only relevant when mode=sticky), strictProxy (boolean), proxyPoolIds (string[] - ordered list of proxy pool IDs in this group), isActive (boolean), createdAt, updatedAt. Export them from src/lib/localDb.ts barrel.
Step 2: Add proxyGroupAccess.ts in src/lib/ (following the same pattern as src/lib/proxyPoolAccess.ts) with async wrapper functions for the CRUD operations.

Step 3: Add API routes for proxy groups at src/app/api/proxy-groups/route.ts (GET list, POST create) and src/app/api/proxy-groups/[id]/route.ts (GET detail, PUT update, DELETE). Follow the same auth/validation patterns as src/app/api/proxy-pools/route.ts. The GET list endpoint should support ?includeUsage=true to show how many providers/connections use each group. Ensure a 'default' group is auto-created on first GET if none exists.

Step 4: Extend settings.providerProxyDefaults to support a new field: proxyGroupId (in addition to existing proxyPoolId). When proxyGroupId is set, it takes precedence over proxyPoolId. Update src/app/api/settings/route.ts to handle providerProxyDefaults with proxyGroupId field.
Step 5: Extend providerSpecificData to support proxyGroupId field for per-connection group assignment. Update the providers/[id]/route.ts PUT handler to accept proxyGroupId similarly to proxyPoolId.

Step 6: Rewrite src/lib/network/connectionProxy.ts resolveConnectionProxyConfig to add group resolution. New resolution chain: 1) Check connection-level proxyGroupId -> resolve group -> pick proxy from group. 2) Check connection-level proxyPoolId -> use directly. 3) Check provider-default proxyGroupId -> resolve group -> pick proxy. 4) Check provider-default proxyPoolId -> use directly. 5) Return none. Add a new function resolveProxyFromGroup(groupId) that: loads the group, filters to active pools, implements round-robin cursor (in-memory Map<groupId, index>) or sticky logic (track last-used pool + count per group in-memory).

Step 7: Add API endpoint src/app/api/proxy-groups/[id]/remove-all/route.ts (POST) that removes the group assignment from ALL connections and provider defaults that reference this group. This is the 'Remove all proxy for all accounts' action.

Step 8: Write unit tests in tests/unit/proxy-group-resolution.test.ts that verify: group creation, round-robin rotation across pools, sticky mode staying on same pool within limit, fallback to direct when strictProxy=false and proxy fails, error when strictProxy=true and proxy fails, resolution chain priority (connection group > connection pool > provider group > provider pool > none).
acceptance_criteria: ProxyGroup CRUD works via API endpoints with proper validation
A 'default' group is auto-created on first access if none exists
Round-robin mode rotates through active proxy pools in the group on each resolution call
Sticky mode reuses the same proxy pool up to stickyLimit times before rotating
Resolution chain respects priority: connection proxyGroupId > connection proxyPoolId > provider default proxyGroupId > provider default proxyPoolId > none
strictProxy=true on group causes request failure instead of direct fallback
Remove-all endpoint clears all references to a group across connections and settings
Unit tests pass for all resolution scenarios
findings: All verification steps passed (lint, typecheck, test, build). Node 22 installed via mise. 189 test files pass (1072 tests). User sent steering about health checks/auto-test and account overrides display -- these are separate features outside FEAT-001 scope.
